description: private constructor + companion object factory method `create` enforces invariants before constructing a Gifticon, preventing invalid instances from being built directly
Brand is an immutable data class that wraps brand name with validation — a canonical DDD value object with no identity of its own
ExpiryDate wraps LocalDate with domain-meaningful behavior (isExpiringSoon, isExpired) and guards creation with past-date validation — a value object encapsulating expiry domain logic
Gifticon is an aggregate root that controls its own state transitions (markUsed, markExpired) and encapsulates child value objects (Brand, ExpiryDate) while enforcing ownership invariants
ShareGroup is an aggregate root that manages its ShareMember collection, enforces uniqueness invariants on membership, and gates access through owner/member checks
GifticonEvent uses a sealed class hierarchy to represent all possible domain events as a closed, exhaustively matchable type set

codeSnippet: enum class GifticonStatus { ACTIVE, USED, EXPIRED }
description: GifticonStatus models the finite state machine of a gifticon's lifecycle as an enum, enabling exhaustive when-expression matching for state transitions

Domain-specific exception hierarchy extends a shared BusinessException base with typed ErrorCode, enabling structured error handling across layers without leaking infrastructure concerns into the domain
ExpiryAlertPolicy is declared as a Kotlin object (singleton) to represent a stateless, shareable domain policy with no need for multiple instances

codeSnippet:
data class ViewMark(
    val gifticonId: Long,
    val userId: Long,
    val viewedAt: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.now()
)
description: ViewMark uses data class for structural equality, copy(), and destructuring — appropriate for a read-tracking record that acts as an immutable value object

codeSnippet:
interface GifticonRepositoryPort {
    fun save(gifticon: Gifticon): Gifticon
    fun findById(id: Long): Gifticon?
    fun findAllByOwnerId(ownerId: Long, pageable: Pageable): Page<Gifticon>
    fun findExpiringGifticons(targetDate: LocalDate): List<Gifticon>
    fun findExpiredActiveGifticons(today: LocalDate): List<Gifticon>
    fun delete(id: Long)
}
description: GifticonRepositoryPort is a hexagonal architecture outbound port — an interface defined in the application layer that is implemented by the infrastructure adapter (GifticonRepositoryAdapter)
ImageStoragePort defines the storage abstraction boundary so the application layer never depends on local filesystem details — a secondary (driven) port in hexagonal architecture
PushNotificationPort abstracts the push delivery mechanism so the domain can trigger notifications without coupling to any specific push service implementation

GifticonRepositoryAdapter implements GifticonRepositoryPort, adapting Spring Data JPA to the domain interface — a classic hexagonal infrastructure adapter that translates between JPA entities and domain objects
PushNotificationAdapter implements the PushNotificationPort interface, adapting external push delivery to the application's notification contract
LocalImageStorageAdapter implements ImageStoragePort for local filesystem, adapting file I/O operations to the domain's storage abstraction

RegisterGifticonUseCase is a primary (driving) port in hexagonal architecture — a use case interface with nested Command/Result DTOs that defines the application boundary for the presentation layer to drive

GifticonService implements multiple use case interfaces from the application layer and depends only on port abstractions — enforcing clean architecture's dependency rule: outer layers depend inward, never the reverse
GifticonService implements six use case interfaces simultaneously, demonstrating Kotlin's multiple-interface implementation to keep each use case as a discrete contract
Business logic (status transition guards, ownership check) lives inside the domain entity itself — a hallmark of DDD rich domain model rather than anemic model

GifticonJpaEntity uses JPA annotations (@Entity, @Table, @Enumerated) to map the domain aggregate to a relational table, with explicit toDomain/fromDomain conversion to maintain separation from the domain model
ShareGroupJpaEntity uses @OneToMany with CascadeType.ALL and orphanRemoval to manage the member collection lifecycle through JPA's ORM cascade semantics
GifticonController sits in the presentation layer and depends only on use case interfaces — never on service implementations or repositories, enforcing strict layered architecture boundaries
GifticonController exposes a RESTful API with standard HTTP verbs (POST, GET, PUT, DELETE) mapped to resource-oriented endpoints, returning ApiResponse wrappers with appropriate HTTP status codes
requireNotNull is used consistently after save() calls to convert nullable id (Long?) into non-null Long with a descriptive error, leveraging Kotlin's null safety contract at the application boundary

codeSnippet: val gifticonId = gifticon.id ?: return
description: Elvis operator with early return safely handles nullable id without throwing, demonstrating Kotlin null-safe flow control
`also` scope function is used to update fields on an existing JPA entity inline while returning the entity itself, keeping the mutation logic co-located with the retrieval
`apply` scope function initializes the RoutingDataSource configuration inline, returning the configured instance without a separate variable — idiomatic Kotlin builder-style configuration
Lambda expressions with `map` and `filter` are used throughout the service layer for transforming domain collections into result DTOs without imperative loops
@Transactional and @Transactional(readOnly=true) annotations declaratively manage transaction boundaries, separating write and read-only transaction semantics at the service level
RoutingDataSource routes read-only transactions to a REPLICA and write transactions to MASTER, implementing the read/write separation aspect of CQRS at the infrastructure level
companion object hosts the static `fromDomain` factory function for JPA entity construction, keeping JPA-specific mapping logic out of the domain model while scoping it to the entity class
SharedGifticonPort abstracts the sharing persistence mechanism behind a capability-driven interface, allowing application logic to reason about sharing without knowing the underlying storage strategy

private constructor prevents direct instantiation of Gifticon from outside the class, forcing all creation through the factory methods that enforce invariants — core encapsulation of construction logic
MarkViewed checks for an existing ViewMark before saving, making the operation idempotent — repeated calls from the same user on the same gifticon have no additional effect
The share operation checks for an existing record before inserting, ensuring duplicate share calls are idempotent and won't create duplicate SharedGifticon records
runCatching { }.getOrNull() is used as an inline try-catch idiom to safely parse the expiry date string, returning null on failure rather than throwing — idiomatic Kotlin null-safe error handling

GifticonApplication is an independently deployable Spring Boot service within an MSA platform, restricted to its own bounded context package with shared common libraries loaded via explicit scan paths

codeSnippet:
package com.kgd.gifticon.domain.gifticon.model

import com.kgd.gifticon.domain.gifticon.exception.InvalidGifticonStatusException
import java.time.LocalDate
import java.time.LocalDateTime
description: The gifticon domain is isolated under com.kgd.gifticon with no cross-service imports, representing a self-contained bounded context within the MSA platform
